LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANTS BERLITZ LANGUAGES, INC., ELS EDUCATIONAL SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT ("ACC") IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STATUTORY INDEMNITY, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED IT WAS AN AGENT OF CROSS-DEFENDANTS (ACC 12), AND THAT IT PROVIDED CORPORATE SERVICES TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS IN GOOD FAITH AND IN THEIR BEST INTERESTS. (ACC 13). AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR IMPLIED AND EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. EACH ALLEGES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONTRIBUTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED BECAUSE CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT PLED THAT A MONEY JUDGMENT WAS RENDERED JOINTLY AGAINST TWO OR MORE DEFENDANTS. (SEE GENERAL ELECTRIC CO. V. STATE OF CA (1973) 32 CAL. APP. 3RD 918, 925.) CROSS-DEFENDANTS DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED; CROSS-COMPLAINANT PLEADS AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY BETWEEN THE PARTIES. THE SPECIAL DEMURRER FOR UNCERTAINTY IS OVERRULED. CROSS-DEFENDANTS CAN DETERMINE WHAT CLAIMS ARE DIRECTED AGAINST THEM. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIF., INC. (1993) 14 CAL. APP. 4TH 612, 616.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ERIK WEISS, ESQ., 415.788.1900, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT OEC INTERNATIONAL TRAVELER'S CLUB, LLC.
ERIC SCHAEFER, ESQ., 626.993.1590, FOR CROSS DEFENDANTS BERLITZ LANGUAGES, INC. AND ELS EDUCATIONAL SERVICES, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANTs BERLITZ LANGUAGES, INC., ELS EDUCATIONAL SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT ("ACC") is overruled as to the first, second, third and fifth causes of action and sustained as to the fourth cause of action. As to the first cause of action for statutory indemnity, Cross-Complainant has sufficiently pled it was an agent of Cross-Defendants (ACC 12), and that it provided corporate services to Cross-Defendants in good faith and in their best interests. (ACC 13). As to the second and third causes of action for implied and equitable indemnity, the demurrer is overruled. Each alleges facts sufficient to state a cause of action. As to the fourth cause of action for contribution, the demurrer is sustained because Cross-Complainant has not pled that a money judgment was rendered jointly against two or more defendants. (See General Electric Co. v. State of CA (1973) 32 Cal. App. 3rd 918, 925.) Cross-Defendants demurrer to the fifth cause of action is overruled; Cross-Complainant pleads an actual controversy between the parties. The special demurrer for uncertainty is overruled. Cross-Defendants can determine what claims are directed against them. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal. App. 4th 612, 616.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
